markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Verify Tiptap/ProseMirror JSON roundtrips every custom node through backend save/load and CRDT promotion. This MT belongs to SecurityPortabilityValidation. Prove WP-009 behavior with negative fixtures, visual debugging, portability checks, and validator-ready evidence.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
Validation must include negative fixtures for false PASS, stale state, missing Postgres, missing assets, secret leakage, partial PDF extraction, parallel write conflicts, and UI overlap.
Portability checks must reject hardcoded machine paths and authority stored only in generated projections.
scope.expected_behavior: TiptapRoundtripFixture leaves a concrete, restartable implementation or activation artifact with PostgreSQL/EventLedger authority, Master Spec anchors, UserManual impact, and validator-readable evidence.
acceptance_criteria: TiptapRoundtripFixture is implemented or specified with enough detail for a no-context local model to continue from the MT alone.
The MT names exact product/governance files, state tables or APIs, constraints, failure modes, and verification evidence.
The MT rejects sidecar research-paper authority and outside-runtime proof; accepted research ideas are restated as Handshake-native requirements.
The MT handoff names HBR rows proven, blocked, or not applicable with concrete reasons.
verification: Parse the WP/MT contract and confirm this MT keeps all required fields populated.
Run the smallest relevant product, governance, or fixture test available for the touched surface; if not implemented yet, define the exact future test and negative case.
For UI/editor/UserManual surfaces, use visual debugging or declare why no GUI surface exists for this MT.
Confirm no SQLite, Docker-default, external-app, external-daemon, sidecar-paper, or mock-only proof satisfies the MT.
handoff_requirements: List files changed or intentionally left untouched.
List commands/tests run and exact pass/fail/blocker status.
List EventLedger/PostgreSQL evidence, receipt IDs, fixtures, visual-debug artifacts, and repair queues produced or expected.
List remaining risks, compatibility shims, deprecated wording, and next MTs unblocked.

behavior_delivered: Backend fixture proves custom Tiptap/ProseMirror nodes survive PostgreSQL create/save/load/history.
Backend fixture proves CRDT update/snapshot promotion metadata, snapshot IDs, and promotion receipt event IDs survive save/load/history.
Backend fixture proves block-tree parsing, stable JSON roundtrip, inline hsLink/tagMention retention, typed backlink extraction, and persisted backlink rows.
Frontend export/import coverage proves data-hs-* semantic attributes are consumed when legacy render attributes are absent.
Frontend export coverage fails closed for missing or oversized media instead of emitting backend content URLs into self-contained exports.
MT-236 fixture now fails closed when managed PostgreSQL binaries are unavailable instead of returning a green skipped test.

verification_runs.command: $env:HANDSHAKE_MANAGED_PG_BIN='definitely-not-a-real-pg-bin-dir-xyz'; Remove-Item Env:POSTGRES_TEST_URL -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Remove-Item Env:DATABASE_URL -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test knowledge_tiptap_roundtrip_fixture_tests -- --nocapture
verification_runs.result_before_fix: FAILED REQUIREMENT: command exited 0 and reported 1 passed while printing SKIP markers for missing PostgreSQL.

verification_runs.command: $env:HANDSHAKE_MANAGED_PG_BIN='definitely-not-a-real-pg-bin-dir-xyz'; Remove-Item Env:POSTGRES_TEST_URL -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; Remove-Item Env:DATABASE_URL -ErrorAction SilentlyContinue; cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml --features test-utils --test knowledge_tiptap_roundtrip_fixture_tests -- --nocapture
verification_runs.result_after_fix: PASS-AS-NEGATIVE: command exited 1; test panicked with MT-236 requires PostgreSQL proof; missing PostgreSQL must fail closed.

verification_runs.result: PASS: 1 test; custom nodes survive save/load/history and CRDT promotion. Rust compile emitted existing warnings.

verification_runs.result: PASS: 1 Playwright test; RichTextEditor, Monaco code block, typed wikilinks, schema rehydrate roundtrip, worker chunk fetch, and zero external requests. Vite chunk-size warnings only.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Tiptap/ProseMirror JSON roundtrips every custom node through backend save/load and CRDT promotion.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: knowledge_tiptap_roundtrip_fixture_tests passes against PostgreSQL/EventLedger-backed rich document and CRDT paths.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: Negative fixtures prevent false PASS and missing PostgreSQL proof.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Missing managed-PG binary probe now exits nonzero instead of returning green SKIP.
Offline Playwright rich_editor_roundtrip proof passed and saved screenshot artifact.

acceptance_mapping.criterion: No mock-only, sidecar, SQLite, Docker-default, or external-app proof satisfies MT-236.
acceptance_mapping.evidence: Backend fixture uses PostgreSQL helper and now fails hard if PostgreSQL is unavailable; Playwright network gate blocks non-loopback requests.

notes: No lifecycle PASS status was written; MT-236 remains READY_FOR_VALIDATION for independent validator action.
The broader product worktree remains dirty with unrelated WP-KERNEL-009 work; this evidence is scoped to MT-236 and the fail-closed fixture repair.

findings.finding: MT-236 fixture previously returned success when managed PostgreSQL binaries were missing.
findings.fix: Changed the fixture to require knowledge_pg().await and panic on None, making missing PostgreSQL a failing proof path.

findings.finding: MT-236 lifecycle was READY_FOR_VALIDATION with null completed_by and no lifecycle.evidence array.
findings.fix: Added lifecycle evidence and completed_by while preserving READY_FOR_VALIDATION status.

diff_attack_surfaces: Serialization/persistence of every custom rich-editor node through backend save/load/history.
CRDT snapshot/update metadata and promotion receipt event IDs.
Fail-closed behavior for media/embed export errors.
independent_checks_run: Adversarial reviewer ran independent hs_link_node, monaco_code_block_node, and embed_assets vitests: 3 files / 35 tests passed.
Adversarial reviewer probed invalid POSTGRES_TEST_URL and observed nonzero exit.
Adversarial reviewer probed missing managed-PG binaries and found a false-pass before the fix.
Main agent reran the missing managed-PG binary probe after the fix and observed nonzero exit.
counterfactual_checks: If loaded.content_json equality or BlockTree::from_document_json were removed, custom node stripping could pass unnoticed.
If hs_link_node.ts stopped reading data-hs-ref-* attributes, primary HTML export/import would lose typed link semantics.
If the MT-236 fixture returned on knowledge_pg() == None, missing PostgreSQL infrastructure would again report a green skipped proof.
boundary_probes: Producer/consumer: export_formats.ts stamps data-hs-* attributes; hs_link_node.ts and monaco_code_block_node.ts parse those attributes.
Backend writer/reader: create/save/load/list version history all assert content_json plus CRDT snapshot and receipt metadata.
negative_path_checks: Missing managed PostgreSQL binaries now fail the fixture command.
Invalid explicit PostgreSQL URL exits nonzero per adversarial probe.
Frontend export tests cover invalid refs, missing assets, oversized images/videos, and oversized slideshow sequences.
residual_uncertainty: Rust compile still emits unrelated pre-existing warnings.
Playwright proof is an offline built-harness proof for UI/runtime behavior; backend authority proof is covered by the separate PostgreSQL fixture.

wp_validator_review_v2.review_method: Integration Validator multi-agent workflow (wf_0ac25815-485 + wf_98386044-70a): independent per-MT reviewer + adversarial confirm on FAIL. No ACP. Governance/repo paperwork non-blocking per operator directive (2026-06-18). Bar = good code vs MT contract + WP packet + current Master Spec v02.194, plus the WP_VALIDATOR Spec-Realism Gate.
wp_validator_review_v2.summary: MT-236 requires a Tiptap/ProseMirror roundtrip fixture proving every custom editor node survives real backend save/load/version-history and CRDT promotion, with fail-closed negative behavior and no mock/SQLite proof. The committed fixture delivers exactly that against real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL, and aligns with the current Master Spec's Tiptap/ProseMirror + PostgreSQL-authority rich editor.
findings: src/backend/handshake_core/tests/knowledge_tiptap_roundtrip_fixture_tests.rs:40-111 builds a doc covering all 20+ custom node kinds (heading, bulletList, orderedList, codeBlock, monacoCodeBlock, table, taskList, blockquote, image/video/album/slideshow, fileLink/folderLink/projectLink/specLink/wpLink/symbolLink, importedRaw, plus inline hsLink across 11 refKinds, mention, tagMention).

Fixture lines 232-359: real PostgreSQL create_knowledge_rich_document -> save_knowledge_rich_document_version(v2) preserves content_json, crdt_snapshot_id, promotion_receipt_event_id; lines 397-440 prove idempotent save v3 with replayed==true on second call; lines 442-454 load asserts doc_version==3 and exact content_json.

Lines 456-502: BlockTree::from_document_json parses all 20 blocks in exact expected order, tree.to_document_json()==loaded.content_json (stable roundtrip), and inline hsLink/tagMention nodes are asserted not stripped.
Lines 504-558: DocumentLinkReferences::extract + replace_knowledge_document_backlinks persists typed file/wp/wikilink/mention/tag backlinks to a real backend table and asserts each persisted.
Lines 560-592: list_knowledge_rich_document_versions returns 3 versions each with correct content_json, crdt_snapshot_id, and promotion_receipt_event_id — real version history proof.

src/backend/handshake_core/tests/knowledge_pg_support.rs:1-120 documents and implements REAL-PostgreSQL-only proof via Handshake-native ManagedPostgres (port 5544, repo-local managed_pgdata); explicitly NO SQLite/in-memory/mock; returns None only when PG binaries absent.

Sub-rule 1: grep of fixture, pg helper, and app/src/lib/editor/export_formats.ts found no todo!/unimplemented!/unreachable!/'not yet wired'/'pending MT-'/Unavailable placeholders reachable from the proof path.

Fail-closed repair confirmed: fixture line 233-235 knowledge_pg().await.expect("MT-236 requires PostgreSQL proof; missing PostgreSQL must fail closed") panics (exit nonzero) instead of green-skipping when PG is missing — matches the claimed RED->GREEN missing-Postgres negative path.

Backend methods are real, not test-authored mocks: storage/knowledge.rs:2976/3018/3218 (and PostgresDatabase impls at 4882/5117/5999) implement create/save/idempotent-save; BlockKind variants (block_tree.rs:68-145) and DocumentLinkKind::Wikilink (backlink.rs:26-82) are real enum variants with real parsing.

app/src/lib/editor/export_formats.ts:196-313 implements real fail-closed media export — fetches against real backend metadata/content endpoints, enforces size guards, marks data-hs-export-error and emits no backend content URL into self-contained exports on missing/oversized/failed media.
Spec alignment: v02.194 07-user-experience-and-development.md confirms Tiptap/ProseMirror as canonical rich editor and PostgreSQL authority — exactly what the fixture proves.

Aside (paperwork only, non-blocking): lifecycle.implementation_commit is null though the fixture is committed at c49afb7f; packet spec_anchors cite v02.192 while current is v02.194 — both are expected governance drift per ground rules.
evidence_checked: git log feat/WP-KERNEL-009 (commit c49afb7f touching the fixture)

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
